title: Use :telemetry.attach_many/4 with pattern-matching handle_event clauses
prompt: Write `MyApp.ObanMetrics` with an `attach/0` function that uses `:telemetry.attach_many/4` (not `attach/4` in a loop) to register one handler for three events: `[:oban, :job, :start]`, `[:oban, :job, :stop]`, `[:oban, :job, :exception]`. Define `handle_event/4` with three separate function clauses, one per event. Start clause increments a start counter, stop clause records duration, exception clause increments an error counter. Use `MyApp.Metrics.increment/1` and `MyApp.Metrics.timing/2`. Write to `lib/my_app/oban_metrics.ex`.
tier_rationale: Tests pattern-match dispatch in handle_event + the `attach_many/4` API shape. Both are documented but easy to miss.
